cooper-voice-from-the-south.alt: Studio portrait of Anna Julia Cooper as a young woman, late 1880s
cooper-voice-from-the-south.caption: Anna Julia Cooper, photographed in Oberlin, Ohio around 1888, the educator and scholar whose 1892 A Voice from the South argued for Black women's higher education and leadership.
cooper-voice-from-the-south.repository: Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division (LC-DIG-ppmsca-50303)
cooper-voice-from-the-south.license: Public domain (published before 1929; no known restrictions on publication)

wells-the-red-record.alt: Head-and-shoulders portrait of Ida B. Wells, 1891
wells-the-red-record.caption: Ida B. Wells in 1891, around the time she launched the anti-lynching documentation campaign that produced The Red Record (1895).
wells-the-red-record.photographer: Unknown (published in I. Garland Penn, The Afro-American Press and Its Editors, 1891)
wells-the-red-record.repository: Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division (LCCN 93505758)
wells-the-red-record.license: Public domain (published before 1929; no known restrictions on publication)

negro-problem-industrial-education-washi.alt: Hampton Institute students constructing a house, 1899
negro-problem-industrial-education-washi.caption: Hampton Institute students at work on a house they largely built themselves, photographed by Frances Benjamin Johnston in 1899, the kind of trades-based industrial education Booker T. Washington championed.
negro-problem-industrial-education-washi.repository: Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division (LCCN 00652583)
negro-problem-industrial-education-washi.license: Public domain (published before 1929; no known restrictions on publication)

dubois-souls-alexander-crummell.alt: Portrait of the Reverend Alexander Crummell
dubois-souls-alexander-crummell.caption: Alexander Crummell, the Episcopal minister and scholar whose life Du Bois memorialized in The Souls of Black Folk, from the frontispiece of his 1882 sermon collection.
dubois-souls-alexander-crummell.photographer: Unknown (frontispiece of Alexander Crummell, The Greatness of Christ and Other Sermons, 1882)
dubois-souls-alexander-crummell.repository: Internet Archive (book digitization), via Wikimedia Commons

dubois-souls-booker-t-washington.alt: Portrait of Booker T. Washington, circa 1895
dubois-souls-booker-t-washington.caption: Booker T. Washington around 1895, photographed by Frances Benjamin Johnston, the leader whose accommodationist program Du Bois critiqued in The Souls of Black Folk.
dubois-souls-booker-t-washington.repository: Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division (ppmsca.23961)
dubois-souls-booker-t-washington.license: Public domain (published before 1929; no known restrictions on publication)
